Professor View 01 / 37
Travel English — 10 Aulas

Tânia Rosa

De viajante que trava na hora H a comunicadora autônoma que circula pelo mundo com confiança

A2 Perfil Cosmopolita 60 min / Online 3x por semana
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Tânia Rosa
50 anos
Perfil cosmopolita, alta mobilidade geográfica
A2 (Pré-Intermediário)
Travel English — Conversação prática e pronúncia
10 aulas de 60 min
3x por semana — Online (Zoom)
Online

Jornada de Transformação

De: Viajante experiente que já morou em outros países, mas ainda trava no vocabulário e na pronúncia quando mais precisa.

Para: Comunicadora autônoma que circula pelo mundo com segurança, conversa com pessoas de diferentes culturas e lidera as viagens da família em inglês — em 10 aulas.

Promessa do Programa

"Quer poder se comunicar em inglês sem ter muitas dificuldades." — Em 10 aulas, Tânia vai de viajante que já se comunica mas trava na hora H a comunicadora confiante que conversa com qualquer pessoa, em qualquer país, sem depender de ninguém.

Forças

  • Base comunicativa funcional — já se expressa em situações simples
  • Exposição informal por imersão — morou em outros países
  • Motivação autêntica — inglês conectado ao estilo de vida
  • Abertura cultural e experiência internacional
  • Energia positiva e receptividade ao aprendizado

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Vocabulário limitado em situações intermediárias
  • Pronúncia de expressões mais complexas
  • Fluência oral impactada por lacunas de vocabulário
  • Variabilidade emocional frente a erros

Currículo Completo — 10 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
1Who Is Tânia? — Diagnostic + Personal IntroductionPresent Simple (affirmative, negative, questions)Self-introduction role-play + Dialogue on planeRecord 1-min self-introduction in English
2At the Airport — Navigating International TerminalsCan/Could for requests, airport vocabularyCheck-in simulation + Boarding pass readingListen to airport announcements podcast
3Hotel Check-In — Booking and Settling InWould like, reservation vocabularyHotel reception role-playWrite 5 hotel-related sentences
4Getting Around — Directions and TransportationImperative, prepositions of placeMap reading + Asking directions role-playWatch city tour video, note 5 expressions
5At the Restaurant — Ordering and the BillCould I have, food vocabularyMenu reading + Ordering role-playRecord ordering at a restaurant
6Shopping and Souvenirs — Prices and BargainingHow much, comparativesShopping dialogue + Price negotiationList 10 things to buy abroad with prices
7Cultural Conversations — Places, People, TraditionsPresent Perfect (been to), cultural vocabularyCultural exchange role-playWatch documentary, write 3 observations
8Emergency Situations — Health, Safety, HelpNeed to, have to, emergency vocabularyEmergency scenarios + Pharmacy role-playCreate personal emergency phrase card
9Family Travel — Coordinating with FamilySuggestions (let us, shall we), family vocabularyFamily trip planning role-playRecord 1-min about favorite family trip
10Putting It All Together — Review and ConfidenceAll structures reviewedFull travel simulation: airport to sightseeingFinal reflection recording
Aula 01 — Pre-class

Who Is Tânia?

Diagnostic Session and Personal Introduction for the World Traveler
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de se apresentar em inglês, falar sobre suas viagens e manter uma conversa simples com um desconhecido.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Travel·viajar
"I love to travel to new places."
Destination·destino
"My next destination is Portugal."
Passport·passaporte
"Could I see your passport, please?"
Experience·experiência
"Living abroad is a great experience."
Culture·cultura
"I enjoy learning about different cultures."
Visit·visitar
"I want to visit Japan next year."
Country·país
"Brazil is a beautiful country."
Language·idioma / língua
"English is an international language."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Travel
Destination
Passport
Experience
Culture
Visit
Country
Language

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia Rosa lives in Brazil. She travels to many countries every year. She loves visiting new destinations and learning about different cultures. Her family does not speak English at home, but Tânia wants to improve. Does she enjoy visiting new places? Yes, she does. She loves learning about cultures.

1. What does Tânia do every year?
A She stays home.
B She travels to many countries.
C She studies French.
2. Does her family speak English at home?
A Yes, they do.
B No, they do not.
C Only on weekends.
3. Does Tânia enjoy visiting new places?
A Yes, she does.
B No, she does not.
C She prefers staying home.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Present Simple — Presente Simples

Usamos o Present Simple para falar de fatos, hábitos e rotinas. / We use the Present Simple for facts, habits, and routines.

FormStructureExample
Affirmative (afirmativa)Subject + verb (+s/es)She travels every year.
Negative (negativa)Subject + do/does not + verbShe does not speak French.
Question (pergunta)Do/Does + subject + verb?Does she like traveling?
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"She travel every year." ✗
"She travels every year." ✓
He, she, it = verbo + S. Sempre!

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

"I usually with my family."
"She speak French very well."
" you visit other countries often?"
"He learning new languages."
"We love different cultures."
"Tânia Rosa to new destinations every year."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem certa para montar uma apresentação pessoal completa.

?"I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
?"Hi, I am Tânia Rosa. Nice to meet you."
?"I want to improve my English for traveling."
?"I am from Brazil. I love to travel."
?"I usually travel with my children and grandchildren."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

Hi, I am Tânia Rosa. Nice to meet you.
I love to travel. I have been to many countries.
My family and I visit new places together.
I want to speak English with confidence.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You meet someone at the airport. They ask: "Where are you from?" What do you say?
A "I want go Brazil."
B "I am from Brazil. Nice to meet you."
C "Brazil is hot."
D "I no speak English."
Someone asks: "Do you travel often?" You love traveling. What do you say?
A "No, I does not."
B "I travel much yes."
C "Yes, I do. I love to travel."
D "Travel is good."
You want to ask someone about their job. What is the correct question?
A "What is your work?"
B "What do you do?"
C "You work what?"
D "Does you work?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are on a plane to your dream destination. The person next to you asks: "Tell me about yourself." What do you say? Talk about your name, where you are from, what you love doing, and why you travel.

Survival Card — Lesson 1

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1Hi, I am Tânia Rosa. I am from Brazil.Oi, eu sou a Tânia Rosa. Eu sou do Brasil.
2I love traveling. I have been to many countries.Eu adoro viajar. Já estive em muitos países.
3Could you repeat that, please?Poderia repetir, por favor?
4I enjoy visiting new countries and cultures.Eu gosto de visitar novos países e culturas.
5I usually travel with my children and grandchildren.Eu geralmente viajo com meus filhos e netos.

O que eu aprendi — Aula 1

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 02 — Pre-class

At the Airport

Navigating International Terminals — Check-in, Gates, and Boarding
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de fazer check-in no aeroporto, encontrar seu portão de embarque e lidar com qualquer situação em inglês, usando pedidos educados com "Could I...?"

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Flight·voo
"My flight to London departs at 10 AM."
Boarding Pass·cartão de embarque
"Could I see your boarding pass, please?"
Gate·portão de embarque
"Your gate is B12."
Luggage·bagagem
"I need to check my luggage."
Check-in·registro no balcão do aeroporto
"Could I check in for my flight, please?"
Terminal·terminal (prédio principal do aeroporto)
"The international terminal is on the left."
Departure·partida
"The departure time is 3:30 PM."
Arrival·chegada
"The arrival time in Paris is 8 PM."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Flight
Boarding Pass
Gate
Luggage
Check-in
Terminal
Departure
Arrival

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is at the airport. She is checking in for her flight to Paris. At the counter, she asks: "Could I have a window seat, please?" The agent says: "Can I see your boarding pass?" Tânia could not find her gate at first, but an agent helped her. She asks: "Could you tell me where Terminal B is?" Her departure time is 3:30 PM from gate B7.

1. What does Tânia ask for at the counter?
A A window seat.
B A meal on the plane.
C A new passport.
2. Could Tânia find her gate easily?
A Yes, she found it immediately.
B No, she could not find it at first.
C She did not go to the gate.
3. What time is the departure?
A 2:00 PM.
B 3:30 PM.
C 8:00 PM.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Can / Could — Pedidos Educados (Polite Requests)

Usamos "Can" e "Could" para fazer pedidos educados. "Could" é mais formal e polido que "Can". / We use "Can" and "Could" for polite requests. "Could" is more formal and polite than "Can".

FormStructureExample
Request (pedido)Could I + verb...?Could I have a window seat, please?
Asking for help (pedir ajuda)Can/Could you + verb...?Can you help me with my luggage?
Negative (negativa)Subject + could not + verbI could not find my gate.
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"I want a window seat." ✗
"Could I have a window seat, please?" ✓
Use "Could I...?" para pedir com educação. NUNCA "I want" em pedidos!

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

" I have a window seat, please?"
"I find my gate."
"Could you tell me where my is?"
"The time is 3:30 PM."
"Can you help me with my ?"
"The time in Paris is 8 PM."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem certa para montar um check-in completo no aeroporto.

?"Could I have a window seat, please?"
?"Good morning! I am checking in for my flight to Paris."
?"Could you tell me where gate B7 is?"
?"Here is my passport and booking confirmation."
?"Yes, I have one suitcase to check."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

Could I check in for my flight, please?
Could I have a window seat, please?
Could you tell me where Terminal B is?
Could you help me with my luggage, please?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You want a window seat. How do you ask politely?
A "I want a window seat."
B "Could I have a window seat, please?"
C "Give me a window seat."
D "Window seat now."
You cannot find your gate. What do you say?
A "Where gate?"
B "I want find gate."
C "Excuse me, could you tell me where gate B7 is?"
D "Gate B7 where?"
The agent asks: "Can I help you?" What do you say?
A "No."
B "Yes, please. I am looking for Terminal B."
C "I want Terminal B."
D "Terminal where?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are at the airport check-in counter. The agent asks for your passport and booking confirmation. You want a window seat, and you need to know where your gate is. What do you say? Practice the full conversation using "Could I...?" and "Could you...?"

Survival Card — Lesson 2

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1Could I check in for my flight, please?Poderia fazer o check-in do meu voo, por favor?
2Could I have a window seat, please?Poderia ter um assento na janela, por favor?
3Where is gate B7?Onde é o portão B7?
4Could you help me with my luggage, please?Poderia me ajudar com minha bagagem, por favor?
5Could you tell me the departure time?Poderia me dizer o horário de partida?

O que eu aprendi — Aula 2

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 03 — Pre-class

Hotel Check-In

Booking and Settling into Your Home Abroad
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de fazer check-in em um hotel, perguntar sobre o quarto e lidar com qualquer situação de hospedagem em inglês, usando pedidos educados com "I would like..." e "Could I...?"

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Reservation·reserva
"I have a reservation under the name Rosa."
Room·quarto
"Could I have a room with a view, please?"
Key·chave (do quarto)
"Here is your room key. You are in room 405."
Checkout·horário de saída do hotel
"Checkout is at 11 AM."
Receptionist·recepcionista
"The receptionist was very helpful."
Elevator·elevador
"The elevator is on your right."
Breakfast·café da manhã
"Would you like breakfast included?"
Floor·andar (de um prédio)
"Your room is on the fourth floor."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Reservation
Room
Key
Checkout
Receptionist
Elevator
Breakfast
Floor

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia arrives at the Grand Palace Hotel after a long flight. She goes to the reception desk. She tells the receptionist: "I have a reservation under the name Rosa." The receptionist checks the system and finds her booking. Tânia says: "I would like a room with a view, if possible." The receptionist offers room 405 on the fourth floor. He asks: "Would you like breakfast included?" Tânia answers: "Yes, I would." He gives her the key and says: "The elevator is on your right. Checkout is at 11 AM."

1. What room does Tânia get?
A Room 301 on the third floor.
B Room 405 on the fourth floor.
C Room 210 on the second floor.
2. Does Tânia want breakfast included?
A Yes, she would.
B No, she would not.
C She did not answer.
3. What time is checkout?
A 10 AM.
B 11 AM.
C 12 PM.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Would Like — Pedidos e Preferências Educados (Polite Requests and Preferences)

Usamos "Would like" para expressar desejos e preferências de forma educada. NUNCA use "I want" em pedidos! / We use "Would like" to express wishes and preferences politely. NEVER use "I want" in requests!

FormStructureExample
Request (pedido)I would like + noun...I would like a room with a view, please.
Question (pergunta)Would you like + noun...?Would you like breakfast included?
Negative (negativa)I would not like + noun...I would not like a room near the elevator.
Short answer (resposta curta)Yes, I would. / No, thank you.Yes, I would. / No, thank you.
Compare with Aula 2:
Could I = asking permission/ability. "Could I have a window seat?"
Would like = expressing preference/desire politely. "I would like a room with a view."
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"I want a room." ✗
"I would like a room, please." ✓
Use "I would like..." para expressar desejos com educação. NUNCA "I want" em pedidos!

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

"I a room with a view, please."
" you like breakfast included?"
"I have a under the name Rosa."
" is at 11 AM."
"The is on your right."
"Your room is on the fourth ."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem certa para montar um check-in completo no hotel.

?"I would like a room with a view, please."
?"What time is checkout?"
?"Good evening! I have a reservation under the name Rosa."
?"Yes, I would. What time is breakfast served?"
?"Yes, a double room for three nights."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

I have a reservation under the name Rosa.
I would like a room with a view, please.
What time is breakfast served?
Could you recommend a good restaurant nearby?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You arrive at the hotel. How do you tell the receptionist you have a booking?
A "I want my room now."
B "I have a reservation under the name Rosa."
C "Give me room."
D "Room please now."
You want a room with a nice view. How do you ask politely?
A "I want room with view."
B "View room now."
C "I would like a room with a view, please."
D "Give me view room."
The receptionist asks: "Would you like breakfast included?" You want it. What do you say?
A "I want breakfast."
B "Yes, I would. Thank you."
C "Breakfast now."
D "Yes want."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you arrive at a luxury hotel in Paris. The receptionist greets you. Tell them you have a reservation, ask for a room with a view, ask about breakfast, and ask about checkout time. Use "I would like..." and "Could you...?" to be polite.

Survival Card — Lesson 3

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1I have a reservation under the name Rosa.Eu tenho uma reserva no nome Rosa.
2I would like a room with a view, please.Eu gostaria de um quarto com vista, por favor.
3What time is breakfast served?A que horas é servido o café da manhã?
4What time is checkout?A que horas é o checkout?
5Could you recommend a good restaurant nearby?Poderia recomendar um bom restaurante por perto?

O que eu aprendi — Aula 3

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 04 — Pre-class

Getting Around the City

Asking for Directions and Using Transportation
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de pedir direções na rua, entender instruções de como chegar a um lugar e usar transporte público em inglês, usando imperativos e preposições de lugar.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Direction·direção
"Could you tell me the direction to the museum?"
Street·rua
"The restaurant is on Main Street."
Corner·esquina
"Turn right at the corner."
Block·quarteirão
"The pharmacy is two blocks from here."
Subway·metrô
"Could you tell me where the subway station is?"
Map·mapa
"I need a map of the city."
Bridge·ponte
"Walk across the bridge and turn left."
Sidewalk·calçada
"Stay on the sidewalk and go straight."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Direction
Street
Corner
Block
Subway
Map
Bridge
Sidewalk

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia leaves her hotel and wants to visit the Art Museum. She stops a local person on the street. She asks: "Excuse me, could you help me? I am looking for the Art Museum." The local says: "Go straight on this street for two blocks. Then turn left at the corner." Tânia asks: "Is it far?" The local answers: "No, the museum is across from the bridge, next to a café. You can also take the subway. The station is on the next block." Tânia says: "Thank you! I will walk on the sidewalk. I have a map too."

1. Where is the Art Museum?
A Next to the hotel, on the right.
B Across from the bridge, next to a café.
C Inside the subway station.
2. How many blocks does Tânia walk straight?
A One block.
B Two blocks.
C Three blocks.
3. What does Tânia turn at the corner?
A Right.
B Left.
C She goes straight.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Imperative + Prepositions of Place (Imperativo + Preposições de Lugar)

O imperativo é usado para dar instruções diretas. Preposições de lugar indicam posição. / The imperative gives direct instructions. Prepositions of place indicate position.

TypeStructureExample
ImperativeGo / Turn / Walk / TakeGo straight for two blocks.
next toao lado deThe museum is next to the café.
betweenentreThe bank is between the café and the hotel.
across fromdo outro lado deThe museum is across from the bridge.
on the corner ofna esquina deThe pharmacy is on the corner of Main Street.
in front ofem frente aThe bus stop is in front of the hotel.
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"Go to the left" ✗ → "Turn left" ✓
"The bank is in the corner" ✗ → "The bank is on the corner" ✓
Use "Turn left/right" (nunca "Go to the left"). Use "ON the corner" (nunca "IN the corner").

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

" straight for two blocks."
" left at the corner."
"The museum is the café."
"The bank is the bridge."
"The pharmacy is two from here."
" across the bridge and turn left."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem certa para dar direções ao museu.

?"Then turn left at the corner."
?"You can also take the subway."
?"Excuse me, could you help me? I am looking for the Art Museum."
?"The museum is across from the bridge, next to a café."
?"Go straight on this street for two blocks."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

Excuse me, could you help me? I am looking for the Art Museum.
Go straight for two blocks, then turn right.
The museum is across from the bridge, next to a café.
Could you tell me where the subway station is?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You are lost. How do you ask for help?
A "Where museum?"
B "Excuse me, could you help me? I am looking for the Art Museum."
C "Museum now please."
D "Go museum."
Someone tells you "Turn left at the corner." What do you do?
A Go straight and ignore the corner.
B At the corner, turn to the left side.
C Turn right at the corner.
D Stop walking.
You want to find the subway. What do you say?
A "Subway where?"
B "I want subway."
C "Excuse me, could you tell me where the subway station is?"
D "Give subway."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are walking in a new city and you are lost. You need to find the Art Museum, a good restaurant, and the subway station. Ask a local person for directions to all three places. Use "Excuse me...", "Could you tell me...", "How do I get to...", and prepositions of place.

Survival Card — Lesson 4

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1Excuse me, could you help me?Com licença, poderia me ajudar?
2How do I get to the Art Museum?Como eu chego ao Museu de Arte?
3Turn left at the corner and go straight.Vire à esquerda na esquina e siga em frente.
4Is it far to walk?É longe para ir a pé?
5Could you tell me where the subway station is?Poderia me dizer onde fica a estação de metrô?

O que eu aprendi — Aula 4

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 05 — Pre-class

At the Restaurant

Ordering Food and Handling the Bill
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de pedir comida em um restaurante, perguntar sobre o cardápio e lidar com a conta em inglês, usando "Could I have..." e vocabulário de restaurante.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Menu·cardápio
"Could I see the menu, please?"
Order·pedir / pedido
"Are you ready to order?"
Appetizer·entrada
"I would like an appetizer to start."
Main Course·prato principal
"For the main course, I would like the grilled fish."
Dessert·sobremesa
"Could I see the dessert menu, please?"
Bill·conta
"Could I have the bill, please?"
Tip·gorjeta
"Is the tip included in the bill?"
Waiter·garçom
"Excuse me, could I ask the waiter a question?"

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Menu
Order
Appetizer
Main Course
Dessert
Bill
Tip
Waiter

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia arrives at a restaurant called La Bella Vista. The waiter says: "Welcome! A table for one?" Tânia says: "Could I sit near the window?" The waiter brings the menu. Tânia looks at the appetizers, the main courses, and the desserts. She says: "Could I have the tomato soup as an appetizer?" For the main course, she says: "I would like the grilled fish." After the meal, she asks: "Could I have the bill, please?" She also asks: "Is the tip included?" The waiter smiles and says: "Yes, it is. Thank you!"

1. What does Tânia order as an appetizer?
A The grilled fish.
B The tomato soup.
C The Caesar salad.
2. What structure does Tânia use to order politely?
A "I want the fish."
B "Could I have the fish, please?"
C "Give me the fish."
3. Is the tip included in the bill?
A Yes, it is.
B No, it is not.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

"Could I have..." for Polite Ordering (Pedidos educados no restaurante)

Use "Could I have..." ou "I would like..." para pedir de forma educada. NUNCA use "I want" em restaurantes — é rude em inglês. / Use "Could I have..." or "I would like..." for polite ordering. NEVER use "I want" at restaurants — it sounds rude.

StructureExampleTradução
Could I have + nounCould I have the grilled fish, please?Poderia me trazer o peixe grelhado?
Could I see + nounCould I see the menu?Poderia ver o cardápio?
Could we have + nounCould we have a table for two?Poderíamos ter uma mesa para dois?
Could you + verbCould you bring the bill, please?Poderia trazer a conta?
I would like + nounI would like the grilled fish.Eu gostaria do peixe grelhado.
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"I want the fish." ✗ → "Could I have the fish, please?" ✓
"Give me the bill." ✗ → "Could I have the bill, please?" ✓
Use "Could I have..." or "I would like..." (nunca "I want" ou "Give me").

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

" the bill, please?"
" the grilled fish with vegetables, please."
"Could I see the , please?"
"Is the included in the bill?"
"I would like an to start."
"For the , I would like the grilled fish."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta de um pedido no restaurante.

?"I would like the grilled fish with vegetables, please."
?"Could I have the bill, please?"
?"Good evening! Could I have a table for one, please?"
?"Could I see the dessert menu, please?"
?"Could I see the menu, please?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

Could I have the grilled fish, please?
I would like a glass of sparkling water, please.
Could I have the bill, please?
Is the tip included in the bill?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You arrive at a restaurant. How do you ask for a table?
A "Table now."
B "Good evening! Could I have a table for one, please?"
C "I want a table."
D "Give me table."
The waiter asks: "Are you ready to order?" You want fish. What do you say?
A "Fish."
B "Yes, I would like the grilled fish, please."
C "I want fish now."
D "Give fish."
You finished eating. How do you ask for the bill?
A "Money please."
B "I want to pay."
C "Could I have the bill, please?"
D "Give bill."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are at a beautiful restaurant abroad. You want to order an appetizer, a main course, a dessert, and a drink. Order everything politely using "Could I have..." and "I would like...". Then ask for the bill and ask about the tip.

Survival Card — Lesson 5

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1Could I see the menu, please?Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?
2I would like the grilled fish, please.Eu gostaria do peixe grelhado, por favor.
3Could I have the bill, please?Poderia me trazer a conta, por favor?
4Is the tip included?A gorjeta está incluída?
5Thank you, the food was delicious!Obrigada, a comida estava deliciosa!

O que eu aprendi — Aula 5

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 06 — Pre-class

Shopping and Souvenirs

Prices, Sizes, and Bargaining
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de fazer compras no exterior — perguntar preços, tamanhos, comparar itens e pagar em inglês, usando "How much" e comparativos.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Price·preço
"What is the price of this scarf?"
Size·tamanho
"Do you have this in a larger size?"
Souvenir·lembrança de viagem
"I would like to buy some souvenirs for my grandchildren."
Cash·dinheiro (papel e moedas)
"Could I pay in cash?"
Credit Card·cartão de crédito
"Do you accept credit cards?"
Discount·desconto
"Is there a discount if I buy two?"
Receipt·recibo / comprovante
"Could I have the receipt, please?"
Change·troco
"Here is your change. Thank you!"

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Price
Size
Souvenir
Cash
Credit Card
Discount
Receipt
Change

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is at a souvenir shop called Bella Souvenirs. She sees a beautiful blue scarf and asks: "How much is this scarf?" The shopkeeper says: "Thirty-five euros." Tânia also sees a red scarf and asks: "Is the red one cheaper?" The shopkeeper says: "Yes, the red one is twenty-eight euros. It is smaller, but very popular." Tânia wants both and asks: "Is there a discount if I buy two?" The shopkeeper gives her a good deal: fifty-five euros instead of sixty-three. Tânia says: "Could I pay with a credit card?" She gets her receipt and leaves happy with her souvenirs.

1. How much is the blue scarf?
A Twenty-eight euros.
B Thirty-five euros.
C Fifty-five euros.
2. Which scarf is cheaper?
A The blue scarf.
B The red scarf.
C They cost the same.
3. How does Tânia pay?
A In cash.
B With a credit card.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

"How much" + Comparatives (Preços e comparações)

Use "How much is/does" para perguntar preços. Use comparativos para comparar itens: adjetivos curtos + -er (cheap → cheaper) ou more + adjetivos longos (expensive → more expensive). / Use "How much is/does" for prices. Use comparatives: short adj + -er (cheap → cheaper) or more + long adj (expensive → more expensive).

StructureExampleTradução
How much is + nounHow much is this scarf?Quanto custa este lenço?
How much does + noun + costHow much does the bag cost?Quanto custa a bolsa?
Short adj + -er + thanThis one is cheaper than that one.Este é mais barato que aquele.
more + long adj + thanThe blue scarf is more expensive than the red one.O lenço azul é mais caro que o vermelho.
bigger / smaller / betterDo you have this in a bigger size?Você tem isso em um tamanho maior?
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"How much it costs?" ✗ → "How much does it cost?" ✓
"This is more cheap." ✗ → "This is cheaper." ✓
Lembre: How much + does/is (auxiliar obrigatório). Short adjectives use -er, not "more".

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

" this scarf?"
"This one is than that one."
"Do you have this in a larger ?"
"Is there a if I buy two?"
"Could I have the , please?"
"The blue scarf is than the red one."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta de uma compra na loja.

?"Is it cheaper than the blue one?"
?"Could I have the receipt, please?"
?"Good afternoon! I am looking for souvenirs."
?"Could I pay with a credit card?"
?"How much is this scarf?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

How much is this scarf?
Do you have this in a larger size?
Is there a discount if I buy two?
Could I have the receipt, please?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You see a beautiful bag at a shop. How do you ask the price?
A "How much it costs?"
B "How much is this bag?"
C "What price bag?"
D "Tell me price."
You want to compare two scarves. One costs 35 euros and the other 28 euros. What do you say?
A "This is more cheap than that one."
B "The red one is cheaper than the blue one."
C "Red is cheap more."
D "The red is less money."
You want to pay. How do you ask if they accept credit cards?
A "Card okay?"
B "I pay card."
C "Do you accept credit cards?"
D "Give me card machine."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are at a souvenir shop abroad. You want to buy gifts for your family — your children and grandchildren. Ask about prices, compare items, ask for a discount, and pay. Use "How much is...?", comparatives (cheaper, more expensive, bigger), and polite payment phrases.

Survival Card — Lesson 6

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1How much is this?Quanto custa isto?
2Do you have this in a larger size?Tem isso em um tamanho maior?
3Is there a discount?Tem desconto?
4Could I pay with a credit card?Posso pagar com cartão de crédito?
5Could I have the receipt, please?Posso ter o recibo, por favor?

O que eu aprendi — Aula 6

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 07 — Pre-class

Cultural Conversations

Talking About Places, People, and Traditions
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de falar sobre suas experiências de viagem, descrever lugares que visitou e ter conversas culturais significativas em inglês, usando o Present Perfect com "been to" e "visited".

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Tradition·tradição — um costume passado de geração em geração
"Every country has its own traditions."
Festival·festival — um evento especial com música, comida ou arte
"I have been to a music festival in Spain."
Monument·monumento — uma construção ou estrutura histórica importante
"The Eiffel Tower is a famous monument."
Local·local — uma pessoa que mora em um lugar / daquele lugar
"The locals were very friendly and helpful."
Custom·costume — uma maneira tradicional de fazer as coisas
"It is a custom to tip the waiter in the United States."
Heritage·patrimônio — a história e tradições de um país ou família
"Brazil has a rich cultural heritage."
Landmark·ponto turístico — um lugar famoso e fácil de reconhecer
"The Colosseum is a landmark in Rome."
Diversity·diversidade — ter muitos tipos diferentes de pessoas ou coisas
"I love the cultural diversity of big cities."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Tradition
Festival
Monument
Local
Custom
Heritage
Landmark
Diversity

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is at a café talking to Marco, the owner. Marco asks: "Have you ever been to Italy?" Tânia says: "Yes, I have been to Rome and Florence. The monuments are incredible." She tells Marco about the traditions and customs she loved. Tânia also says: "I have been to a music festival in Spain." Marco says he has never been to South America. Tânia describes Brazil's diversity and rich heritage. She recommends visiting famous landmarks like Rio de Janeiro and Salvador. The locals in every region are very friendly.

1. Which countries has Tânia visited?
A Japan and China.
B Italy and Spain.
C England and France.
2. Has Marco been to South America?
A Yes, he has.
B No, he has never been there.
C The text does not say.
3. What does Tânia recommend Marco to visit in Brazil?
A Museums and restaurants.
B Rio de Janeiro and Salvador.
C The Amazon only.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Present Perfect com "been to" e "visited" (Experiências de viagem)

Use o Present Perfect para falar de experiências de vida (viagens, lugares visitados). Estrutura: Subject + have/has + past participle. "Been to" = visitou um lugar e voltou. / Use the Present Perfect to talk about life experiences (travel, places visited). Structure: Subject + have/has + past participle. "Been to" = visited a place and returned.

StructureExampleTradução
AffirmativeI have been to Portugal.Eu já estive em Portugal.
NegativeI have never been to Asia.Eu nunca estive na Ásia.
QuestionHave you ever visited Japan?Você já visitou o Japão?
3rd personShe has been to many countries.Ela já esteve em muitos países.
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"I have been in Portugal." ✗ → "I have been to Portugal." ✓
"Did you ever visit Japan?" ✗ → "Have you ever visited Japan?" ✓
Lembre: "been to" (não "been in") para lugares visitados. Use "Have/Has" (não "Did") com o Present Perfect.

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

"I Portugal, Spain, and France."
" Japan?"
"She has Asia."
"Brazil has a rich cultural ."
"The Colosseum is a famous in Rome."
"Every country has its own ."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta de uma conversa cultural.

?"I loved the traditions and the food."
?"You should visit Rio de Janeiro and Salvador."
?"Have you ever been to Italy?"
?"Brazil has incredible diversity."
?"Yes, I have been to Rome and Florence."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France.
Have you ever visited Brazil?
Brazil has a rich cultural heritage.
I love the cultural diversity of big cities.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

Someone asks if you have been to Europe. What do you say?
A "I was in Europe last year."
B "Yes, I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
C "I go to Europe."
D "Europe is nice."
You want to ask someone about their travel experience. What do you say?
A "Did you ever visit Brazil?"
B "Have you ever visited Brazil?"
C "You visit Brazil?"
D "Do you visited Brazil?"
How do you say you have never been somewhere?
A "I never have been to Asia."
B "I have never been to Asia."
C "I not been to Asia."
D "I have been never to Asia."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are at a cultural event abroad. Talk about the countries you have visited, the traditions you love, and the landmarks you want to see. Use "I have been to...", "Have you ever visited...?", "I have never been to...", and vocabulary like tradition, heritage, diversity, landmark, and custom.

Survival Card — Lesson 7

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France.Eu já estive em Portugal, Espanha e França.
2Have you ever visited Brazil?Você já visitou o Brasil?
3I love learning about different traditions and cultures.Eu amo aprender sobre tradições e culturas diferentes.
4Brazil has a very rich cultural heritage.O Brasil tem um patrimônio cultural muito rico.
5You should visit Rio de Janeiro. It is a famous landmark.Você deveria visitar o Rio de Janeiro. É um ponto turístico famoso.

O que eu aprendi — Aula 7

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 08 — Pre-class

Emergency Situations

Health, Safety, and Asking for Help
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de lidar com emergências no exterior — pedir ajuda, descrever sintomas e comunicar-se com médicos e farmacêuticos em inglês, usando "need to" e "have to" para obrigações e necessidades.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Emergency·emergência — uma situação séria que precisa de ajuda imediata
"In an emergency, call 112 in Europe."
Pharmacy·farmácia — uma loja onde você compra remédios
"I need to find a pharmacy nearby."
Medicine·remédio — algo que você toma quando está doente
"I need to buy some medicine for my headache."
Doctor·médico — uma pessoa que ajuda quando você está doente
"I need to see a doctor, please."
Pain·dor — uma sensação desagradável no corpo
"I have a pain in my stomach."
Symptom·sintoma — um sinal de que você está doente
"My symptoms are a headache and a sore throat."
Ambulance·ambulância — um veículo que leva pessoas doentes ao hospital
"Could you call an ambulance, please?"
Insurance·seguro — um documento que ajuda a pagar cuidados médicos
"I have travel insurance. Here is my card."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its definition.

Emergency
Pharmacy
Medicine
Doctor
Pain
Symptom
Ambulance
Insurance

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is not feeling well during her trip to Portugal. She needs to find a pharmacy. At the pharmacy, she tells the pharmacist about her symptoms: a headache and a sore throat. The pharmacist says she has to take the medicine twice a day, after meals. Tânia asks: "Do I need to see a doctor?" The pharmacist says: "If you do not feel better in two days, you need to see a doctor." Tânia also shows her insurance card. She feels better knowing she can handle an emergency abroad.

1. What are Tânia's symptoms?
A A stomachache and a fever.
B A headache and a sore throat.
C A broken arm and a cold.
2. How often does Tânia have to take the medicine?
A Once a day.
B Twice a day, after meals.
C Three times a day.
3. When does Tânia need to see a doctor?
A Immediately.
B If she does not feel better in two days.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Need to / Have to (Obrigações e Necessidades)

Use "need to" para necessidades pessoais (algo que você precisa fazer). Use "have to" para obrigações e requisitos (algo que é exigido). Ambos são seguidos de verbo na forma base. / Use "need to" for personal necessities. Use "have to" for obligations and requirements. Both are followed by the base verb.

StructureExampleTradução
need to + verbI need to see a doctor.Eu preciso ver um médico.
have to + verbYou have to take this medicine twice a day.Você tem que tomar este remédio duas vezes por dia.
NegativeYou do not need to go to the hospital.Você não precisa ir ao hospital.
QuestionDo I have to take it with food?Eu tenho que tomar com comida?
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"I need see a doctor." ✗ → "I need to see a doctor." ✓
"I have take medicine." ✗ → "I have to take medicine." ✓
Lembre: sempre use "to" depois de "need" e "have" quando significam obrigação. Need TO see. Have TO take.

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

"I see a doctor."
"You take this medicine twice a day."
"She rest for two days."
"Do I go to the hospital?"
"I need to find a nearby."
"My are a headache and a sore throat."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta de uma visita à farmácia.

?"I have a headache and a sore throat."
?"Do I have to take it with food?"
?"Good morning. I need some help, please."
?"You need to take this medicine twice a day."
?"What are your symptoms?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Ouvir primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

I need to see a doctor, please.
I have a headache and a sore throat.
Could you call an ambulance, please?
I have travel insurance. Here is my card.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You feel sick abroad. What do you say to find help?
A "I want a pharmacy."
B "Excuse me, I need to find a pharmacy nearby."
C "Give me medicine."
D "Where is hospital?"
The pharmacist asks how to take the medicine. What is correct?
A "You need take it twice."
B "You have to take it twice a day."
C "You have take it twice."
D "You must to take it twice."
How do you ask if you need a prescription?
A "I need prescription?"
B "Do I need a prescription for this medicine?"
C "Have I to get prescription?"
D "Need I prescription?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are traveling abroad and you feel sick. Go to a pharmacy, describe your symptoms, ask about medicine, and ask if you need to see a doctor. Use "I need to...", "Do I have to...?", and vocabulary like symptom, medicine, pharmacy, pain, and insurance.

Survival Card — Lesson 8

These 5 phrases are your emergency kit. Practice them out loud!

1I am not feeling well. I need to see a doctor.Não estou me sentindo bem. Preciso ver um médico.
2I have a headache and a sore throat.Tenho dor de cabeça e dor de garganta.
3I need some medicine, please.Preciso de remédio, por favor.
4Could you call an ambulance, please?Poderia chamar uma ambulância, por favor?
5I have travel insurance. Here is my card.Tenho seguro viagem. Aqui está meu cartão.

O que eu aprendi — Aula 8

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 09 — Pre-class

Family Travel

Coordinating with Family
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula: Ao final, Tânia será capaz de coordenar uma viagem em família em inglês — fazer sugestões, planejar atividades e organizar o roteiro com os parentes, usando "let's", "shall we", "why don't we" e "how about".

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Relatives·parentes — os membros da sua família
"All my relatives are coming on the trip."
Grandparents·avós — os pais dos seus pais
"Let's visit our grandparents this summer."
Cousin·primo(a) — o filho do seu tio ou tia
"My cousin lives in another city."
Niece·sobrinha — a filha do seu irmão ou irmã
"My niece is only six years old."
Nephew·sobrinho — o filho do seu irmão ou irmã
"My nephew loves the beach."
Schedule·programação — um plano de horários para as atividades
"Let's check the schedule for tomorrow."
Sightseeing·passeio turístico — visitar lugares interessantes como turista
"Shall we go sightseeing on Sunday?"
Itinerary·roteiro — o plano dos lugares e horários de uma viagem
"Let's plan the itinerary together."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its translation.

Relatives
Grandparents
Cousin
Niece
Nephew
Schedule
Sightseeing
Itinerary

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is planning a family trip to the coast. She calls her sister Sofia. "Let's plan our trip together," she says. "Shall we invite all our relatives?" Sofia loves the idea. "Why don't we visit our grandparents on the way?" Tânia suggests. They decide to bring their cousins, nieces, and nephews. "How about going sightseeing on Sunday?" Sofia asks. Finally, they check the schedule and plan the itinerary for the whole family.

1. Who is Tânia planning the trip with?
A Her doctor.
B Her sister, Sofia.
C Her neighbor.
2. Who do they want to visit on the way?
A Their friends.
B Their grandparents.
C Their teachers.
3. What do they check at the end?
A The weather report.
B The schedule and the itinerary.
C Their insurance card.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Aqui está a regra. Leia com calma e veja os exemplos.

Making Suggestions (Sugestões)

Usamos estas estruturas para SUGERIR algo a outra pessoa. "Let's", "Shall we" e "Why don't we" vêm seguidos do verbo na forma base. "How about" vem seguido do verbo com -ing. / Use these structures to make suggestions. "Let's", "Shall we" and "Why don't we" are followed by the base verb. "How about" is followed by the verb + -ing.

StructureExampleTradução
Let's + verb"Let's visit our grandparents."Vamos visitar nossos avós.
Shall we + verb?"Shall we go sightseeing?"Vamos fazer um passeio turístico?
Why don't we + verb?"Why don't we book the hotel?"Por que não reservamos o hotel?
How about + verb-ing?"How about visiting the museum?"Que tal visitar o museu?
Common Mistake / Erro Comum:
"Let's to go sightseeing." ✗ → "Let's go sightseeing." ✓
"How about visit the museum?" ✗ → "How about visiting the museum?" ✓
Lembre: depois de "Let's / Shall we / Why don't we" use o verbo na forma base (go, visit). Depois de "How about" use o verbo com -ing (visiting, going).

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

" visit our grandparents this summer."
" go sightseeing on Sunday?"
" book the hotel today?"
"How about the museum?"
" plan the itinerary together."
" invite all our relatives?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para planejar uma viagem em família.

?"Why don't we book the hotel?"
?"How about going sightseeing?"
?"Let's plan our family trip."
?"Let's check the schedule."
?"Shall we invite our relatives?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Listen primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

Let's visit our grandparents this summer.
Shall we go sightseeing tomorrow?
Why don't we book the hotel today?
How about planning the itinerary together?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You want to suggest visiting your grandparents. What do you say?
A "We visit grandparents now."
B "Let's visit our grandparents this weekend."
C "You must visit grandparents."
D "Visit grandparents is good."
Which suggestion is grammatically correct?
A "How about go sightseeing?"
B "Shall we going sightseeing?"
C "How about going sightseeing?"
D "Let's to go sightseeing."
How do you suggest booking the hotel as a question?
A "We book hotel?"
B "Why don't we book the hotel today?"
C "Why we not book hotel?"
D "Book hotel shall?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responda como vier.

Imagine you are planning a family trip with a relative. Make at least four suggestions about where to go, who to invite, and what to do. Use "Let's...", "Shall we...?", "Why don't we...?", and "How about ...-ing?" with words like relatives, grandparents, cousins, schedule, sightseeing, and itinerary.

Survival Card — Lesson 9

These 5 phrases help you coordinate any family trip. Practice them out loud!

1Let's plan our family trip together.Vamos planejar nossa viagem em família juntos.
2Shall we invite all our relatives?Vamos convidar todos os nossos parentes?
3Why don't we visit our grandparents?Por que não visitamos nossos avós?
4How about going sightseeing on Sunday?Que tal fazer um passeio turístico no domingo?
5Let's check the schedule and book the hotel.Vamos verificar a programação e reservar o hotel.

O que eu aprendi — Aula 9

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have mastered this lesson.

Aula 10 — Pre-class

Putting It All Together

Review and Confidence
0%
▼

Objetivo desta aula (revisão final): Ao final, Tânia será capaz de lidar com uma viagem inteira em inglês — check-in, hotel, direções, restaurante, compras, cultura, emergências e família — combinando TODAS as estruturas do curso com confiança.

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Review

Vocabulary

Words you already know — one from each part of your journey. Tap Listen to review the pronunciation.

Passport·passaporte — documento oficial para viajar entre países
"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
Boarding pass·cartão de embarque — o documento que permite entrar no avião
"Could I see your boarding pass, please?"
Reservation·reserva — quando você marca um quarto ou mesa com antecedência
"I would like to check in. I have a reservation."
Directions·direções — instruções de como chegar a um lugar
"Could you give me directions to the museum?"
Menu·cardápio — a lista de comidas e bebidas de um restaurante
"Could I have the menu, please?"
Souvenir·lembrança — um presente que você compra de uma viagem
"How much is this souvenir?"
Landmark·ponto turístico — um lugar famoso ou importante de uma cidade
"I have been to many famous landmarks."
Itinerary·roteiro — o plano dos lugares e horários de uma viagem
"Let's plan the itinerary together."

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Vocabulary

Match each word with its translation.

Passport
Boarding pass
Reservation
Directions
Menu
Souvenir
Landmark
Itinerary

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Tânia is on her last trip of the course. At the airport she says: "Here is my passport. Can you help me with my luggage?" At the hotel: "I would like to check in. I have a reservation." In the city she asks: "Could you tell me the way to the museum?" At the restaurant: "Could I have the menu, please?" She buys a souvenir and asks: "How much is this?" She has been to many landmarks. When she feels sick, she says: "I need to find a pharmacy." Finally she tells her friend: "Let's plan our next trip together!"

1. What does Tânia say at the hotel?
A "Give me a room."
B "I would like to check in. I have a reservation."
C "Where is my passport?"
2. How does Tânia ask for directions?
A "Where museum?"
B "Could you tell me the way to the museum?"
C "I want museum now."
3. What does Tânia say when she feels sick?
A "I want doctor."
B "I need to find a pharmacy."
C "Give me medicine."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Review

Grammar

Todas as estruturas do curso, juntas. Leia com calma — você já sabe todas!

Your Whole Course in One Table
LessonStructureExample
1Present Simple"I travel a lot."
2Can / Could (requests)"Can you help me with my luggage?"
3Would like"I would like to check in."
4Imperative + prepositions"Go straight and turn left."
5Could I have...?"Could I have the menu, please?"
6How much / Comparatives"How much is this? It is cheaper."
7Present Perfect (been to)"I have been to Portugal."
8Need to / Have to"I need to find a pharmacy."
9Suggestions"Let's go sightseeing."
Você consegue! Em 10 aulas você passou de travar na hora H para conduzir uma viagem inteira em inglês. Esta aula junta tudo.

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Practice

Cada frase é de uma aula diferente. Faça um de cada vez.

"Here is my . I am flying to Paris."
"I to check in, please."
" the menu, please?"
" is this souvenir?"
"I find a pharmacy."
"Could you tell me the to the museum?"

Stage 2: Put the Trip in Order

Order

Coloque as frases na ordem da viagem — do aeroporto ao passeio.

?"Could you tell me the way to the museum?"
?"Let's go sightseeing."
?"Here is my passport."
?"Could I have the menu, please?"
?"I would like to check in."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Toque em Listen primeiro. Depois grave sua voz. Compare!

I have been to many countries.
Can you help me with my luggage?
I would like a table for two, please.
Let's plan our next trip together.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação da viagem.

At the airport, the agent wants to see your documents. What do you say?
A "Documents here."
B "Here is my passport and my boarding pass."
C "I have passport."
D "You want passport?"
You want to talk about your travel experiences. What is correct?
A "I go to many countries."
B "I have been to many countries."
C "I been many countries."
D "I am go countries."
You want to suggest visiting a landmark with a friend. What do you say?
A "We go landmark."
B "Let's go sightseeing together."
C "You must see landmark."
D "Sightseeing is good."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Sem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Conte sua viagem completa.

Tell the story of a full trip in English, from the airport to sightseeing. Use at least six different structures from the course: check in ("I would like..."), ask for directions ("Could you tell me the way...?"), order food ("Could I have...?"), ask a price ("How much...?"), talk about experiences ("I have been to..."), handle a problem ("I need to..."), and make a suggestion ("Let's...").

Survival Card — Lesson 10 (The Best of the Course)

Your five most useful phrases for any trip. Practice them out loud!

1Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris.Aqui está meu passaporte. Vou voar para Paris.
2I would like to check in, please.Eu gostaria de fazer o check-in, por favor.
3Could I have the menu, please?Poderia me trazer o cardápio, por favor?
4I need to find a pharmacy.Preciso encontrar uma farmácia.
5Let's plan our next trip together.Vamos planejar nossa próxima viagem juntos.

O que eu aprendi — Curso completo

Read each statement out loud. If you can say yes to all of them, you have completed the course!

Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Small Talk -- Conversation with Strangers

Comece conversas leves com desconhecidos durante as viagens: comente algo, faca perguntas e use tag questions para soar natural.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Small Talk Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you start friendly conversations with strangers.

Stranger -- desconhecido / estranho
"I started a chat with a friendly stranger at the cafe."
Icebreaker -- quebra-gelo
"A comment about the weather is a classic icebreaker."
To chat -- conversar / bater papo
"We chatted for ten minutes while waiting in line."
Coincidence -- coincidência
"What a coincidence -- we are both from Brazil!"
In common -- em comum
"We have a lot in common: we both love travel."
Awkward -- constrangedor / sem graça
"The silence felt awkward, so I asked a simple question."
Polite -- educado / cortês
"It is polite to smile and say hello first."
Small world -- como o mundo é pequeno
"You know my cousin? Small world!"
To get along -- se dar bem (com alguém)
"We got along right away and exchanged numbers."
To break the ice -- quebrar o gelo
"A friendly question is the best way to break the ice."
Welcoming -- acolhedor / receptivo
"The people there were so welcoming to visitors."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Stranger
Icebreaker
To chat
Coincidence
In common
Awkward
Polite
To get along
To break the ice
Welcoming

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the conversation and answer the questions.

Tânia is visiting an art gallery abroad. A friendly stranger smiles and says, "Wonderful museum, isn't it?" That simple comment is a perfect icebreaker. Tânia replies and they begin to chat. "Where are you from?" the man asks. "I'm from Brazil," she says. "You enjoy art, don't you?" he laughs. "What a coincidence -- I painted for years!" They have a lot in common, and the silence is no longer awkward. The people there are very welcoming. It really is a small world.

1. How does the stranger break the ice?
A By commenting on the wonderful museum.
B By complaining about the tickets.
C By asking for money.
2. Which sentence is a tag question?
A "Where are you from?"
B "Wonderful museum, isn't it?"
C "I'm from Brazil."
3. Why is it a "small world"?
A The museum is very small.
B They both turn out to share a love of art.
C They live on the same street.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Questions & Tag Questions

Grammar

Para fazer small talk, você combina perguntas diretas (Where are you from?) e tag questions -- aquele "né?" no final da frase. A tag inverte a forma do verbo: frase afirmativa leva tag negativa, e vice-versa. / To make small talk, you mix direct questions with tag questions (the "né?" at the end). The tag reverses the verb: a positive sentence takes a negative tag, and the other way around.

Tipo / TypeEnglishPortuguês
Wh- questionWhere are you from?De onde você é?
Yes/No questionDo you like the city?Você gosta da cidade?
Tag (+ frase / - tag)It's a lovely day, isn't it?É um dia lindo, né?
Tag (- frase / + tag)You aren't from here, are you?Você não é daqui, né?
Tag (verbo principal)You like art, don't you?Você gosta de arte, né?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"It's a beautiful city, ?"
"You're a traveler here, ?"
" are you from?"
"A friendly question is a great way to break the ."
"What a , we are both from Brazil!"
"We have a lot in ."

Stage 2: Order the Conversation

Order

Listen first, then put the small talk in order.

?Next, Tânia says she is from Brazil and they discover a coincidence.
?Finally, they wish each other a lovely day and say goodbye.
?First, a stranger smiles and comments on the wonderful museum.
?After that, they chat about the welcoming people and the beautiful art.
?Then he asks if Tânia is visiting the city as well.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the rising and falling melody of tag questions.

Lovely weather today, isn't it?
Que tempo bom hoje, né?
Where are you from?
De onde você é?
You're a traveler too, aren't you?
Você também é viajante, né?
It was nice chatting with you.
Foi bom conversar com você.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You sit next to a stranger on a long flight. How do you break the ice?
A "Hi! Are you traveling for work or for fun?"
B "Give me your phone number."
C Say nothing for ten hours.
A person says, "Beautiful day, isn't it?" What do you reply?
A "I don't know you."
B "Yes, it really is! Are you from here?"
C "Goodbye."
You want to end a friendly chat politely. What do you say?
A "Stop talking now."
B "It was lovely chatting with you. Have a lovely day!"
C "You are boring."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are waiting in a long line at a museum abroad. Start a small talk conversation with the stranger next to you. Use an icebreaker, ask two questions, and use at least one tag question. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 11

1Lovely weather today, isn't it?Que tempo bom hoje, né?
2Are you visiting the city too?Você também está visitando a cidade?
3What a coincidence!Que coincidência!
4We have a lot in common.Temos muita coisa em comum.
5It was nice chatting with you.Foi bom conversar com você.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Sharing Opinions -- Agreeing and Disagreeing

Compartilhe o que você realmente pensa nas viagens: dê sua opinião, concorde, discorde com educação e diga o que prefere.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Opinion Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you share and react to opinions.

Opinion -- opinião
"In my opinion, this is the best city in Europe."
To agree -- concordar
"I agree with you -- the food here is wonderful."
To disagree -- discordar
"I disagree -- I think the beach is better."
Point of view -- ponto de vista
"From my point of view, mornings are the best time to travel."
To prefer -- preferir
"I prefer quiet cafes to busy restaurants."
Would rather -- preferir (fazer algo)
"I would rather walk than take a taxi."
It depends -- depende
"Beach or city? It depends on the weather."
To be honest -- para ser sincero(a)
"To be honest, I prefer the quiet streets."
I think so -- acho que sim
"Is this restaurant good? I think so."
I'm not sure -- não tenho certeza
"I'm not sure about that -- I see it differently."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Opinion
To agree
To disagree
Point of view
To prefer
It depends
To be honest
I think so
I'm not sure

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the conversation and answer the questions.

After breaking the ice with a friend, Tânia shares her opinion about the trip. "In my opinion, the old town is the best part," she says. Her friend smiles: "So do I!" Then he adds, "But I prefer the museums." Tânia laughs. "I'm not sure about that. To be honest, I would rather walk around the market." They have a different point of view, but they don't argue. "It depends on the day," she says. They agree to do both, and the conversation stays warm and friendly.

1. What is Tânia's opinion of the trip?
A The old town is the best part.
B The trip was a mistake.
C She has no opinion.
2. How does Tânia disagree with her friend?
A She gets angry and leaves.
B Politely: "I'm not sure about that."
C She says nothing.
3. How do they end the conversation?
A They stop talking.
B They agree to do both things.
C They go home early.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Giving Opinions, Agreeing & Disagreeing

Grammar

Para dar sua opinião, comece com "In my opinion" ou "I think". Para concordar rápido, use "So do I" (depois de frase afirmativa) ou "Neither do I" (depois de frase negativa). Para discordar com educação, suavize: "I'm not sure about that". / To give an opinion, start with "In my opinion" or "I think". To agree quickly, use "So do I" (after a positive sentence) or "Neither do I" (after a negative one). To disagree politely, soften it: "I'm not sure about that".

Função / FunctionEnglishPortuguês
Give opinionIn my opinion, the food is great.Na minha opinião, a comida é ótima.
Agree (+)"I love it." -- "So do I.""Eu amo." -- "Eu também."
Agree (-)"I don't like it." -- "Neither do I.""Não gosto." -- "Nem eu."
Disagree (polite)I'm not sure about that.Não tenho certeza disso.
PreferenceI would rather walk.Eu prefiro caminhar.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"In my , this city is wonderful."
"I love the markets." -- "."
"I don't like crowds." -- "."
"I'm not about that."
"I would walk than take a taxi."
"Beach or city? It on the weather."

Stage 2: Order the Conversation

Order

Listen first, then put the opinion exchange in order.

?Next, her friend agrees and adds that the food is wonderful.
?Finally, they agree to visit both places the next day.
?First, Tânia's friend asks what she thinks of the trip.
?After that, they politely disagree about the best neighborhood.
?Then Tânia gives her opinion and says she loves the old town.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the falling tone in opinions.

In my opinion, this is wonderful.
Na minha opinião, isso é maravilhoso.
So do I!
Eu também!
I'm not sure about that.
Não tenho certeza disso.
I would rather walk than take a taxi.
Eu prefiro caminhar a pegar um táxi.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A friend says, "I love this restaurant." You feel the same. What do you say?
A "So do I! The pasta is amazing."
B "Neither do I."
C "I don't know you."
Someone says, "The tour was boring." You disagree, but want to stay friendly.
A "You are wrong."
B "I'm not sure about that. In my opinion, it was great."
C "Be quiet."
A friend asks, "Taxi or walk?" You like walking more. What do you say?
A "I would rather to take a taxi."
B "I would rather walk. It depends, but today the weather is nice."
C "No opinion."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about your last trip or a place you love. Share your opinion: what is the best part and why? Use "In my opinion", say what you prefer with "I would rather", and add one honest detail with "To be honest". Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 12

1In my opinion, this is wonderful.Na minha opinião, isso é maravilhoso.
2So do I!Eu também!
3Neither do I.Nem eu.
4I'm not sure about that.Não tenho certeza disso.
5I would rather walk than take a taxi.Eu prefiro caminhar a pegar um táxi.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Making Plans -- Your Next Trip

Fale sobre seus planos de viagem: o que você vai fazer (going to), decisões de momento (will) e palavras para se preparar.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Planning Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you plan your next trip.

Plan -- plano
"I have a plan for our weekend trip."
To book -- reservar
"I am going to book the flights tonight."
Budget -- orçamento
"My budget for this trip is small, but enough."
Deadline -- prazo
"The deadline to book the cheap tickets is Friday."
To pack -- fazer as malas
"I always pack the night before."
Packing list -- lista de itens para a mala
"My packing list has shoes, a charger and sunscreen."
To save up -- economizar (juntar dinheiro)
"I am going to save up for a trip to Lisbon."
To look forward to -- ansiar por / esperar com vontade
"I look forward to my holidays every year."
Can't wait -- mal posso esperar
"I can't wait to see the beach!"
Brochure -- folheto
"The brochure shows three nice day tours."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Plan
To book
Budget
Deadline
To pack
To save up
To look forward to
Can't wait
Brochure

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Tânia has a plan for her next trip. "I am going to book the flights this week," she says. Her budget is small, so she is going to save up a little more first. The deadline for the cheap tickets is Friday, so she must hurry. She reads a brochure with day tours and makes a packing list. "Don't worry about your bag," her friend says. "I will pack early and help you too." Tânia smiles. "Thank you! I can't wait. I am really looking forward to it."

1. What is Tânia going to do this week?
A Book the flights.
B Cancel the trip.
C Sell her brochure.
2. Why is she going to save up first?
A She has too much money.
B Her budget is small.
C She lost her tickets.
3. What does the friend decide to do in the moment?
A Nothing.
B "I will pack early and help you."
C Stay home.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Future: Going to vs Will

Grammar

Use "going to" + verbo para um plano que você já decidiu antes (I am going to book a hotel). Use "will" + verbo para uma decisão no momento ou uma promessa (OK, I will take a taxi). Lembre: going to precisa do verbo to be (am / is / are); will nunca leva "to" depois. / Use "going to" + verb for a plan you already decided. Use "will" + verb for a decision in the moment or a promise. Remember: "going to" needs the verb to be (am / is / are); "will" never takes "to" after it.

Função / FunctionEnglishPortuguês
Plan (going to)I am going to book a hotel.Eu vou reservar um hotel.
Plan (going to)We are going to save up money.Nós vamos economizar dinheiro.
Quick decision (will)OK, I will take a taxi.Ok, eu vou pegar um táxi.
Promise (will)Don't worry, I will help you.Não se preocupe, eu vou te ajudar.
Negative (going to)I am not going to forget anything.Eu não vou esquecer nada.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I am to book my flights."
"The phone is ringing. I answer it."
"We are going to up money for the trip."
"My for this trip is small."
"I have my ready."
"I can't to see the beach!"

Stage 2: Order the Plan

Order

Listen first, then put the trip plan in order.

?Next, she writes a long packing list so she forgets nothing.
?Finally, they look forward to the trip and can't wait to leave.
?First, Tânia decides on the destination and sets a budget.
?After that, she and her friend plan the days together.
?Then she books her flights early to save money.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. In fast speech, "going to" often sounds like "gonna".

I am going to book my flights.
Eu vou reservar minhas passagens.
I will help you pack.
Eu vou te ajudar a fazer as malas.
My budget is small, but enough.
Meu orçamento é pequeno, mas suficiente.
I can't wait. I am looking forward to it.
Mal posso esperar. Estou ansiosa por isso.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A friend asks about your decided trip plan. How do you answer?
A "I am going to book the flights next week."
B "I will going to book."
C "I book yesterday."
Your friend's bag is heavy. You decide to help right now.
A "I am going to carry it." (you decided long ago?)
B "Don't worry, I will carry it."
C "I carried it."
The cheap tickets close on Friday. What do you say?
A "The budget is on Friday."
B "The deadline is Friday, so I am going to book today."
C "I pack on Friday only."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about your next trip -- real or a dream. Tell me your plan: where you are going to go (use "going to"), how you are going to save up, what you are going to book first, and why you can't wait. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 13

1I am going to book my flights.Eu vou reservar minhas passagens.
2My budget is small, but enough.Meu orçamento é pequeno, mas suficiente.
3I will help you pack.Eu vou te ajudar a fazer as malas.
4The deadline is Friday.O prazo é sexta-feira.
5I can't wait. I am looking forward to it.Mal posso esperar. Estou ansiosa por isso.
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Travel Stories -- A Trip to Remember

Conte histórias de viagem: descreva o que estava acontecendo (past continuous: was/were + -ing) e a surpresa que cortou a cena (when + past simple).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Story Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you tell a travel story.

Adventure -- aventura
"My trip to the mountains was a real adventure."
To get lost -- se perder
"I got lost in the old town, but it was fun."
View -- vista
"The view from the hill was beautiful."
Scenery -- paisagem (natural)
"The scenery along the road was amazing."
Suddenly -- de repente
"I was walking, and suddenly it started to rain."
On my way -- no caminho / a caminho
"On my way to the hotel, I saw a market."
Journey -- jornada / viagem longa
"The journey to the island took six hours."
Memory -- lembrança / memória
"That trip is my favorite memory."
Amazing -- incrível
"The food was amazing, I will never forget it."
To take a photo -- tirar uma foto
"I was taking a photo when the bird flew away."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Adventure
To get lost
View
Scenery
Suddenly
Journey
Memory
Amazing
To take a photo

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Last summer, Tânia had a small adventure. One afternoon she was walking on a quiet path, and she was taking photos of the amazing scenery. The view was beautiful, so she was not looking at the map. Suddenly, she got lost! Then she saw a kind old man who was reading near a tree. He showed her the way back. Now, when she remembers that journey, she always smiles. It is her favorite memory.

1. What was Tânia doing on the quiet path?
A She was walking and taking photos.
B She was sleeping in the hotel.
C She was reading a book.
2. Why did she get lost?
A There was no path.
B She was not looking at the map.
C She lost her phone.
3. What was the old man doing when she saw him?
A He was running away.
B He was reading near a tree.
C He was taking photos too.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Continuous (was / were + -ing)

Grammar

Use o past continuous (was/were + verbo com -ing) para uma ação que estava acontecendo no passado, ou para montar o cenário de uma história. A ação curta que interrompe vai no past simple. Lembre: I/he/she/it = was; we/you/they = were. / Use the past continuous (was/were + verb-ing) for an action in progress in the past, or to set the scene of a story. The short action that interrupts it goes in the past simple. Remember: I/he/she/it = was; we/you/they = were.

Função / FunctionEnglishPortuguês
Action in progress (I/she)I was walking on the beach.Eu estava caminhando na praia.
Action in progress (they)They were singing in the square.Eles estavam cantando na praça.
Interruption (when + past simple)I was sleeping when the phone rang.Eu estava dormindo quando o telefone tocou.
Set the scene (while)While I was walking, I heard music.Enquanto eu caminhava, ouvi música.
NegativeI was not looking at the map.Eu não estava olhando para o mapa.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I walking on a quiet path."
"They taking photos of the view."
"I was reading when the phone ."
" I was walking, I heard music."
"The view was ."
"That trip is my favorite ."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put the travel story in order.

?After that, she suddenly got lost and felt a little scared.
?Finally, he showed her the way, and now that journey is her favorite memory.
?First, Tânia was walking on a quiet path in the mountains.
?Next, she saw a kind old man who was reading near a tree.
?Then she was taking photos of the amazing view, so she was not looking at the map.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. In fast speech, "was" is short and weak (/wuz/).

I was walking on a quiet path.
Eu estava caminhando por uma trilha tranquila.
Suddenly, I got lost.
De repente, eu me perdi.
The scenery was amazing.
A paisagem estava incrível.
It is my favorite memory.
É a minha lembrança favorita.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A friend asks what you were doing at 8 p.m. yesterday. How do you answer?
A "I was watching a film at home."
B "I watching a film."
C "I will watch a film."
You want to describe two background actions in your story.
A "They was singing and dancing."
B "They were singing and dancing."
C "They are sing and dance."
You want to say a long action was cut by a short one.
A "I sleep when the phone was ring."
B "I was sleeping when the phone rang."
C "I was sleep when the phone rang."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a trip you will never forget. Tell me the story: where you were walking, what you were doing (use "was/were + -ing"), what suddenly happened, and why it is your favorite memory. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 14

1I was walking on a quiet path.Eu estava caminhando por uma trilha tranquila.
2Suddenly, I got lost.De repente, eu me perdi.
3The scenery was amazing.A paisagem estava incrível.
4I was sleeping when the phone rang.Eu estava dormindo quando o telefone tocou.
5It is my favorite memory.É a minha lembrança favorita.
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Describing People & Places

Pinte o quadro de uma viagem: descreva pessoas e lugares e coloque os adjetivos na ordem certa (opinion > size > age > color).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Describing Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you describe people and places.

Tall -- alto(a)
"He is a tall man, taller than me."
Curly -- cacheado / encaracolado
"She has beautiful curly hair."
Friendly -- simpático(a) / amigável
"The people here are very friendly."
Narrow -- estreito(a)
"The old streets are very narrow."
Crowded -- cheio(a) / lotado(a)
"The square was crowded in the evening."
Peaceful -- tranquilo(a) / pacífico(a)
"The garden was peaceful in the morning."
Ancient -- antigo(a) / muito velho(a)
"We visited an ancient town by the sea."
