Three levels of practice — guided, semi-free, and free

Role-Play

Role-Play: Buy Souvenirs

You are at a souvenir shop. Ask about prices, sizes, and buy gifts for your family.
how muchcheapermore expensivesizediscountcredit cardreceipt
Role-Play

Role-Play: Wrong Size

You bought something but it is the wrong size. Go back to the shop and exchange it.
exchangewrong sizecould I
Free Practice

Role-Play: Local Market

You are at a local market. Explore different stalls, compare prices, and buy gifts for your children and grandchildren.

Use everything you learned today: price, size, souvenir, cash, credit card, discount, receipt, change, How much, comparatives.

Feedback

Delayed Error Correction

Your teacher will share 3-5 corrections from the role-plays. Repeat each corrected version twice.

Teacher notes corrections here during the role-plays...

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

5 Key Phrases

1
"How much is this?"
2
"Do you have this in a larger size?"
3
"Is there a discount?"
4
"Could I pay with a credit card?"
5
"Could I have the receipt, please?"
Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can shop abroad in English.
I know 8 shopping vocabulary words.
I can use "How much" for prices.
I can use comparatives (cheaper, more expensive).
I can ask about sizes.
I can handle payment in English.
I can bargain politely.
I feel confident about shopping abroad.

Shopping Pro

Badge earned! You can shop at any store in the world.

Lesson Complete

Day 6 — Complete!

Next lesson: Cultural Conversations — Places, People, Traditions

Thank You

See you
next class!

Alumni by Better — Travel English

Lesson 7

Cultural
Conversations

A2 — 60 minutes — Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Welcome back, Tânia!

"Last class you went shopping. What is a souvenir?"
"Do you remember: what is a discount?"
"Today we talk about places and cultures! Where have you been?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Places, People &
Traditions

Talking about the world with confidence

Today's Goal

After this lesson, Tânia will...

...confidently talk about her travel experiences, describe places she has visited, and have meaningful cultural conversations in English.

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Cultural
Vocabulary

Eight essential words for cultural conversations

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

A custom passed from generation to generation
Tradition
A custom passed from generation to generation
"Every country has its own traditions."
A special event with music, food, or art
Festival
A special event with music, food, or art
"I have been to a music festival in Spain."
An important building or structure from history
Monument
An important building or structure from history
"The Eiffel Tower is a famous monument."
A person who lives in a place
Local
A person who lives in a place / from that place
"The locals were very friendly and helpful."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

A traditional way of doing things
Custom
A traditional way of doing things
"It is a custom to tip the waiter in the United States."
The history and traditions of a country or family
Heritage
The history and traditions of a country or family
"Brazil has a rich cultural heritage."
A famous place that is easy to recognize
Landmark
A famous place that is easy to recognize
"The Colosseum is a landmark in Rome."
Having many different types of people or things
Diversity
Having many different types of people or things
"I love the cultural diversity of big cities."
0 / 4 words revealed
Review

Say It All

Click each word to listen. Repeat each one twice.

Tradition"Every country has its own traditions."
Festival"I have been to a music festival in Spain."
Monument"The Eiffel Tower is a famous monument."
Local"The locals were very friendly and helpful."
Custom"It is a custom to tip the waiter in the United States."
Heritage"Brazil has a rich cultural heritage."
Landmark"The Colosseum is a landmark in Rome."
Diversity"I love the cultural diversity of big cities."
Callback

Words You Already Know

From Lesson 6 — you went shopping and now you talk about places and cultures!

PriceHow much something costs
SouvenirSomething you buy to remember a trip
DiscountA reduction in price
Credit CardA plastic card for payment
Chapter 3: The Code

Present Perfect +
Been To

The secret to talking about travel experiences

Grammar Discovery

Grammar Discovery

Look at the colored words. What do they have in common?

I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France.
Have you ever visited Japan?
She has never been to Asia.
We have traveled to many countries.
StructureFormExample
AffirmativeSubject + have/has + past participleI have been to Portugal.
Negativehave/has + never + past participleShe has never been to Asia.
QuestionHave/Has + subject + ever + past participleHave you ever visited Japan?
Been tovisited a place and returnedI have been to many countries.
Common Mistake

Watch Out!

"I have been in Portugal."
"I have been to Portugal."
"Did you ever visit Japan?"
"Have you ever visited Japan?"
Practice

Complete the Sentences

"I ___have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
"___Have you ever visited Japan?"
"She ___has never been to Asia."
"We ___have traveled to many countries."
"Brazil ___has a very rich cultural heritage."
Build

Build Your Cultural Phrases

Use the Present Perfect with "been to":

Say you have been to Italy: "___I have been to Italy."
Ask if someone has visited Brazil: "___Have you ever visited Brazil?"
Say you have never been to Asia: "___I have never been to Asia."
Describe Brazil's heritage: "___Brazil has a very rich cultural heritage."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Tânia at
the Café

A cultural conversation with Marco

Artifact

The Travel Journal

Tânia's Travel Journal

My World Adventures

Countries Visited
  • Portugal — Lisbon and Porto
  • Spain — Barcelona and Madrid
  • France — Paris and Nice
Favorite Traditions
  • Fado music in Portugal
  • La Tomatina festival in Spain
  • Carnival in Brazil
Next Destinations

I would love to visit Japan, Greece, and Morocco.

Best Memory

Walking through the streets of Lisbon at sunset, listening to Fado music in a small restaurant. The locals were incredibly friendly and the food was amazing. Portugal has a beautiful cultural heritage.

Every trip teaches you something new about the world.
Dialogue

At the Café

M
You are Brazilian, right? I love Brazil! Have you ever been to Italy?
T
Yes, I have! I have been to Rome and Florence. The monuments are incredible.
M
What did you like most about Italy?
T
I loved the traditions and the food. Every region has different customs.
M
That is true! Have you visited any festivals in your travels?
T
Yes, I have been to a music festival in Spain. It was an amazing experience.
M
Spain is wonderful! I have never been to South America. What is Brazil like?
T
Brazil has incredible diversity — different cultures, languages, and traditions in every region.
M
That sounds fascinating! What landmarks should I visit?
T
You should visit Rio de Janeiro, the Amazon, and Salvador. Brazil has a very rich heritage.
Comprehension

About the Dialogue

1. Which countries has Tânia visited?
She has been to Italy (Rome and Florence) and Spain (a music festival).
2. Has Marco ever been to South America?
No, he has never been to South America.
3. What does Tânia say about Brazil?
Brazil has incredible diversity and a very rich heritage. She recommends Rio, the Amazon, and Salvador.
Listening

Cultural Conversation

Listen to Tânia and Marco talking about travel and culture.

1. Which countries did they talk about?
Italy, Spain, and Brazil.
2. What does Tânia love about her travels?
The traditions, customs, and cultural diversity.
Listening

Listen for Present Perfect

Listen again. Count how many times they use "have been to" or "have visited".

1. How many times is "have been to" used?
Four times: "Have you ever been to Italy?", "I have been to Rome", "I have been to a festival", "I have never been to South America".
2. Who has never been to South America?
Marco has never been to South America.
Chapter 5: Practice

Time to
Practice

Quick fire, spot the error, and oral drilling

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

Click on each card to reveal the answer.

1. Someone asks if you have been to Europe.
"Yes, I have. I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
2. Ask someone if they have visited Brazil.
"Have you ever visited Brazil?"
3. Say you have never been to Asia.
"I have never been to Asia, but I would love to go."
4. Describe a tradition from your country.
"In Brazil, we have a tradition of celebrating Carnival every year."
5. Someone asks what you liked about a country.
"I loved the local traditions and the cultural diversity."
6. Recommend a landmark.
"You should visit the Eiffel Tower. It is a famous landmark in Paris."
Detective

Spot the Error

"I have been in Portugal."
"I have been to Portugal."
"Did you ever visit Japan?"
"Have you ever visited Japan?"
"She have been to many countries."
"She has been to many countries."
"I never have been to Asia."
"I have never been to Asia."
"The locals was very friendly."
"The locals were very friendly."
0 / 5 errors found
Speaking

Oral Practice

1. Say which countries you have visited.
"I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
2. Ask Marco if he has visited Brazil.
"Have you ever visited Brazil?"
3. Talk about a festival you attended.
"I have been to a music festival in Spain. It was amazing."
4. Describe Brazilian culture.
"Brazil has incredible diversity — different cultures and traditions."
5. Say you have never been somewhere.
"I have never been to Asia, but I would love to visit."
6. Recommend a landmark in Brazil.
"You should visit Rio de Janeiro. It is beautiful."
Build

Fix and Improve

Make these sentences correct:

"I have been in Portugal." ➔ "___I have been to Portugal."
"Did you ever visit Japan?" ➔ "___Have you ever visited Japan?"
"She have been to many countries." ➔ "___She has been to many countries."
Compare

Lesson 6 vs Lesson 7

Lesson 6: How Much + Comparatives

Asking prices and comparing items while shopping

"How much is this? This one is cheaper."

Lesson 7: Present Perfect + Been To

Talking about travel experiences and cultures

"I have been to Portugal. Have you ever visited Brazil?"

Mixed Practice

Shop or Café?

Talk about countries you visited: "___I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
Ask about the price of a souvenir: "___How much is this souvenir?"
Ask if someone has visited a country: "___Have you ever visited Brazil?"
Describe Brazilian culture: "___Brazil has a very rich cultural heritage."
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Time to Speak

Three levels of practice — guided, semi-free, and free

Role-Play

Role-Play: Cultural Chat

You meet Marco at a café. Talk about countries you have visited and cultures you love.
I have been tohave you evertraditionculturediversitylandmarkheritage
Role-Play

Role-Play: Describe Brazil

A tourist asks you about Brazil. Describe your country, its traditions, and recommend places.
you should visitwe haveheritage
Free Practice

Role-Play: Cultural Event

You are at a cultural event abroad. Chat with people from different countries about your travels, traditions, and favorite places.

Use everything you learned today: tradition, festival, monument, local, custom, heritage, landmark, diversity, Present Perfect with been to.

Feedback

Delayed Error Correction

Your teacher will share 3-5 corrections from the role-plays. Repeat each corrected version twice.

Teacher notes corrections here during the role-plays...

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

5 Key Phrases

1
"I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
2
"Have you ever visited Brazil?"
3
"I love learning about different traditions and cultures."
4
"Brazil has a very rich cultural heritage."
5
"You should visit Rio de Janeiro. It is a famous landmark."
Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can talk about my travel experiences.
I know 8 cultural vocabulary words.
I can use Present Perfect with "been to".
I can ask about travel experiences.
I can describe a culture.
I can compare traditions.
I can have a cultural conversation.
I feel confident discussing cultures.

Culture Explorer

Badge earned! You can talk about culture with anyone in the world.

Lesson Complete

Day 7 — Complete!

Next lesson: Emergency Situations — Health, Safety, Help

Thank You

See you
next class!

Alumni by Better — Travel English

Lesson 8

Emergency
Situations

A2 — 60 minutes — Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Words You Already Know

From Lesson 7 — you had wonderful cultural conversations!

TraditionA custom passed from generation to generation
FestivalA special event with music, food, or art
MonumentAn important building or structure from history
LocalA person who lives in a place
Chapter 1: The Dream

Health &
Safety

Handling emergencies with confidence

Today's Goal

After this lesson, Tânia will...

...confidently handle any emergency abroad — ask for help, describe symptoms, and communicate with doctors and pharmacists.

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Emergency
Vocabulary

Eight essential words for health situations

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

A serious situation that needs immediate help
Emergency
A serious situation that needs immediate help
"In an emergency, call 112 in Europe."
A shop where you buy medicine
Pharmacy
A shop where you buy medicine
"I need to find a pharmacy nearby."
Something you take when you are sick
Medicine
Something you take when you are sick
"I need to buy some medicine for my headache."
A person who helps when you are sick
Doctor
A person who helps when you are sick
"I need to see a doctor, please."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

An unpleasant feeling in your body
Pain
An unpleasant feeling in your body
"I have a pain in my stomach."
A sign that you are sick
Symptom
A sign that you are sick
"My symptoms are a headache and a sore throat."
A vehicle that takes sick people to the hospital
Ambulance
A vehicle that takes sick people to the hospital
"Could you call an ambulance, please?"
A document that helps pay for medical care
Insurance
A document that helps pay for medical care
"I have travel insurance. Here is my card."
0 / 4 words revealed
Review

Say It All

Click each word to listen. Repeat each one twice.

Emergency"In an emergency, call 112 in Europe."
Pharmacy"I need to find a pharmacy nearby."
Medicine"I need to buy some medicine for my headache."
Doctor"I need to see a doctor, please."
Pain"I have a pain in my stomach."
Symptom"My symptoms are a headache and a sore throat."
Ambulance"Could you call an ambulance, please?"
Insurance"I have travel insurance. Here is my card."
Bridge

From Culture to Safety

You can talk about traditions and landmarks. But what happens when something goes wrong during a trip?

Chapter 3: The Code

Need to &
Have to

Expressing obligations and necessities

Grammar Discovery

Grammar Discovery

Look at the colored words. What do they have in common?

"I need to see a doctor."
"You have to take this medicine twice a day."
"She needs to rest for two days."
"Do I have to go to the hospital?"
StructureFormExample
need topersonal necessity + base verbI need to see a doctor.
have toobligation/requirement + base verbYou have to take it twice a day.
Negativedo not need to / do not have toYou do not have to go to the hospital.
QuestionDo + subject + need to / have toDo I need to see a doctor?
Common Mistake

Watch Out!

"I need see a doctor."
"I need to see a doctor."
"I have take medicine."
"I have to take medicine."
Practice

Complete the Sentences

"I ___need to see a doctor."
"You ___have to take this medicine twice a day."
"She ___needs to rest for two days."
"Do I ___have to go to the hospital?"
"I ___need to find a pharmacy nearby."
Build

Build Your Emergency Phrases

Use need to and have to:

Say you need to see a doctor: "___I need to see a doctor."
Ask if you have to go to the hospital: "___Do I have to go to the hospital?"
Say she needs to rest: "___She needs to rest for two days."
Ask how to take medicine: "___How do I have to take this medicine?"
Chapter 4: Getting There

At the
Pharmacy

Describing symptoms and buying medicine

Artifact

The Emergency Card

EMERGENCY CARD

Travel Safety

Personal Information

Name: Tânia Rosa
Nationality: Brazilian
Emergency contact: [Family member]
Blood type: ---
Allergies: None reported

Insurance

Travel Insurance — Policy #TR-2026-0520

Emergency Numbers

Europe: 112
Brazil: +55 11 XXXX

Important Phrases

"I need to see a doctor."
"I have travel insurance."

Always carry this card when traveling abroad.
Dialogue

At the Pharmacy

T
Good morning. I need some help, please. I am not feeling well.
P
Good morning! What are your symptoms?
T
I have a headache and a sore throat. I also feel very tired.
P
How long have you had these symptoms?
T
Since yesterday morning. I think I need some medicine.
P
I recommend this pain reliever for the headache. You need to take it twice a day.
T
Do I have to take it with food?
P
Yes, you have to take it after meals. And for the sore throat, try these lozenges.
T
Thank you. Do I need to see a doctor?
P
If you do not feel better in two days, you need to see a doctor. Here is the address of a clinic nearby.
Comprehension

About the Pharmacist

1. What does the pharmacist recommend for the headache?
The pharmacist recommends a pain reliever.
2. How does the pharmacist say to take the medicine?
Twice a day, after meals.
3. When does the pharmacist say to see a doctor?
If she does not feel better in two days.
Listening

At the Pharmacy

Listen to Tânia talking to the pharmacist.

1. What are the symptoms mentioned?
A headache and a sore throat.
2. What does the pharmacist recommend?
A pain reliever for the headache and lozenges for the sore throat.
Listening

Listen for Need to / Have to

Listen again. Count how many times they use "need to" or "have to".

1. How many times is "need to" used?
Four times: "I need some help", "you need to take", "do I need to see", "you need to see".
2. How many times is "have to" used?
Two times: "Do I have to take it with food?", "you have to take it after meals".
Chapter 5: Practice

More
Practice

Quick fire, spot the error, and oral drilling

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

Click on each card to reveal the answer.

1. You feel sick. Ask for a pharmacy.
"Excuse me, I need to find a pharmacy. Is there one nearby?"
2. Describe your symptoms to the pharmacist.
"I have a headache and a sore throat."
3. Ask if you need a prescription.
"Do I need a prescription for this medicine?"
4. Ask how to take the medicine.
"How do I have to take this medicine?"
5. You need to call for help.
"Could you call an ambulance, please? It is an emergency."
6. Show your insurance.
"I have travel insurance. Here is my insurance card."
Detective

Spot the Error

"I need see a doctor."
"I need to see a doctor."
"You have take medicine twice."
"You have to take medicine twice a day."
"I have a pain in the head."
"I have a headache."
"Do I have to going to the hospital?"
"Do I have to go to the hospital?"
"She need to rest."
"She needs to rest."
0 / 5 errors found
Speaking

Oral Practice

1. Tell someone you are not feeling well.
"I am not feeling well. I need to see a doctor."
2. Describe your symptoms.
"I have a headache, a sore throat, and I feel very tired."
3. Ask the pharmacist for medicine.
"I need some medicine for my headache, please."
4. Ask if you need to see a doctor.
"Do I need to see a doctor?"
5. Ask how often to take medicine.
"How often do I have to take this medicine?"
6. Call for help in an emergency.
"Help! Could you call an ambulance, please?"
Build

Fix and Improve

Make these sentences correct:

"I need see a doctor." ➔ "___I need to see a doctor."
"She need to rest." ➔ "___She needs to rest."
"Do I have to going to the hospital?" ➔ "___Do I have to go to the hospital?"
Compare

Lesson 7 vs Lesson 8

Lesson 7: Present Perfect

Talking about travel experiences and cultures

"I have been to Portugal."

Lesson 8: Need to / Have to

Handling health emergencies abroad

"I need to see a doctor."

Mixed Practice

Café or Pharmacy?

Say you need to see a doctor: "___I need to see a doctor, please."
Talk about countries you visited: "___I have been to Portugal, Spain, and France."
Ask how to take medicine: "___How do I have to take this medicine?"
Show your insurance: "___I have travel insurance. Here is my card."
Chapter 6: Production

Show What You Learned

Three levels of practice — guided, semi-free, and free

Role-Play

Role-Play: At the Pharmacy

You go to a pharmacy abroad. Describe your symptoms and buy medicine.
I need toheadachesore throatmedicinetwice a dayhave to
Role-Play

Role-Play: At the Clinic

You are at a clinic. The doctor asks questions about how you feel. Answer and ask about treatment.
symptomsneed tohave totwo days
Free Practice

Role-Play: Help a Traveler

A fellow traveler at your hotel feels sick and does not speak the local language. Help them find a pharmacy, describe their symptoms, and communicate with the pharmacist.

Use everything you learned today: emergency, pharmacy, medicine, doctor, pain, symptom, ambulance, insurance, need to, have to.

Feedback

Delayed Error Correction

Your teacher will share 3-5 corrections from the role-plays. Repeat each corrected version twice.

Teacher notes corrections here during the role-plays...

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

5 Key Phrases

1
"I am not feeling well. I need to see a doctor."
2
"I have a headache and a sore throat."
3
"I need some medicine, please."
4
"Could you call an ambulance, please?"
5
"I have travel insurance. Here is my card."
Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can handle emergencies abroad.
I know 8 emergency vocabulary words.
I can use "need to" and "have to" correctly.
I can describe my symptoms.
I can buy medicine at a pharmacy.
I can communicate with a doctor.
I can help others in emergencies.
I feel confident handling health situations abroad.

Safety Expert

Badge earned! You can handle any health emergency abroad.

Lesson Complete

Day 8 — Complete!

Next lesson: Family Travel — Coordinating with Family

Thank You

See you
next class!

Alumni by Better — Travel English

Lesson 9

Family
Travel

A2 — 60 minutes — Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Before We Travel

From Lesson 8 — say these out loud before we start planning!

Insurance"You need to buy travel insurance before the trip."
Pharmacy"You have to find a pharmacy near the hotel."
need to"I need to see a doctor."
have to"You have to take this medicine twice a day."
Chapter 1: The Dream

A Trip for the
Whole Family

Planning together, suggesting, deciding

Today's Goal

After this lesson, Tânia will...

...confidently plan a family trip in English — make suggestions, invite relatives, and organize the itinerary with everyone.

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Family &
Trip Words

Eight essential words for planning together

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

The members of your family
Relatives
The members of your family
"All my relatives are coming on the trip."
Your parents' parents
Grandparents
Your parents' parents
"Let's visit our grandparents this summer."
The child of your aunt or uncle
Cousin
The child of your aunt or uncle
"My cousin lives in another city."
The daughter of your brother or sister
Niece
The daughter of your brother or sister
"My niece is only six years old."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

The son of your brother or sister
Nephew
The son of your brother or sister
"My nephew loves the beach."
A plan of times for activities
Schedule
A plan of times for activities
"Let's check the schedule for tomorrow."
Visiting interesting places as a tourist
Sightseeing
Visiting interesting places as a tourist
"Shall we go sightseeing on Sunday?"
The plan of places and times for a trip
Itinerary
The plan of places and times for a trip
"Let's plan the itinerary together."
0 / 4 words revealed
Review

Say It All

Click on each word to listen. Repeat each one twice.

Relatives"All my relatives are coming on the trip."
Grandparents"Let's visit our grandparents this summer."
Cousin"My cousin lives in another city."
Niece"My niece is only six years old."
Nephew"My nephew loves the beach."
Schedule"Let's check the schedule for tomorrow."
Sightseeing"Shall we go sightseeing on Sunday?"
Itinerary"Let's plan the itinerary together."
Bridge

From Words to Suggestions

You know the family words. But how do you suggest plans to everyone — and sound friendly and natural?

Chapter 3: The Code

Making
Suggestions

Let's · Shall we · Why don't we · How about

Grammar Discovery

Grammar Discovery

Look at the colored words. What do they have in common?

"Let's visit our grandparents."
"Shall we go sightseeing?"
"Why don't we book the hotel?"
"How about visiting the museum?"
StructureFormExample
Let's+ base verbLet's visit our grandparents.
Shall we+ base verb?Shall we go sightseeing?
Why don't we+ base verb?Why don't we book the hotel?
How about+ verb-ing?How about visiting the museum?
Common Mistake

Watch Out!

"Let's to go sightseeing."
"Let's go sightseeing."
"How about visit the museum?"
"How about visiting the museum?"
Practice

Complete the Suggestions

"___Let's visit our grandparents."
"___Shall we go sightseeing on Sunday?"
"___Why don't we book the hotel today?"
"How about ___visiting the museum?"
"Let's ___plan the itinerary together."
Build

Build Your Suggestions

Use Let's, Shall we, Why don't we, How about:

Suggest inviting the relatives: "___Shall we invite all our relatives?"
Suggest visiting grandparents: "___Let's visit our grandparents."
Suggest booking the hotel: "___Why don't we book the hotel?"
Suggest going sightseeing: "___How about going sightseeing?"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Planning
Together

Tânia and Sofia organize the family trip

Artifact

The Family Itinerary

FAMILY TRIP ITINERARY

The Rosa Family — Coast Trip

Who Is Coming

Tânia & Sofia, parents, grandparents, two cousins, one niece, one nephew

Friday

Travel day. Visit the grandparents on the way.

Saturday

Beach with the nieces and nephews. Family dinner.

Sunday

Sightseeing in the old town. Visit the museum.

Schedule made together — one plan for the whole family.
Dialogue

Planning the Family Trip

T
Sofia, let's plan our family trip to the coast.
S
Great idea! Shall we invite our cousins too?
T
Yes, let's invite all our relatives. How about visiting our grandparents on the way?
S
Perfect. Why don't we book the hotel this week?
T
Good plan. Let's check the schedule for the flights.
S
Shall we go sightseeing when we arrive?
T
Of course. My niece and nephew love the beach.
S
Then let's plan the itinerary together tonight.
T
How about making a list of activities for everyone?
S
Let's do it. This trip will be wonderful for the whole family.
Comprehension

About Sofia

1. What does Sofia suggest inviting?
She suggests inviting their cousins.
2. What does Sofia suggest doing this week?
She suggests booking the hotel this week.
3. What does Sofia want to do when they arrive?
She wants to go sightseeing.
Listening

The Phone Call

Listen to Tânia and Sofia planning the family trip.

1. When do they decide to travel?
In July.
2. Who do they want to bring?
Their parents, grandparents, cousins, nieces, and nephews.
Listening

Listen for Suggestions

Listen again. Count how many suggestions they make.

1. How many suggestions do they make?
Five: "Shall we go in July?", "Let's go in July", "How about a week at the beach?", "Why don't we bring our parents?", "Shall we book two big rooms?"
2. What do they do at the end?
They check the schedule and plan the itinerary.
Chapter 5: Practice

More
Practice

Quick fire, spot the error, and oral drilling

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

Click on each card to reveal the answer.

1. Suggest visiting your grandparents this weekend.
"Let's visit our grandparents this weekend."
2. Ask your sister if you should go sightseeing.
"Shall we go sightseeing on Sunday?"
3. Suggest booking the hotel today.
"Why don't we book the hotel today?"
4. Suggest visiting the museum with the family.
"How about visiting the museum together?"
5. Suggest inviting all the relatives.
"Let's invite all our relatives."
6. Suggest planning the itinerary tonight.
"Shall we plan the itinerary tonight?"
Detective

Spot the Error

"Let's to visit our grandparents."
"Let's visit our grandparents."
"How about go sightseeing?"
"How about going sightseeing?"
"Shall we going to the beach?"
"Shall we go to the beach?"
"Why don't we to book the hotel?"
"Why don't we book the hotel?"
"Let's planning the itinerary."
"Let's plan the itinerary."
0 / 5 errors found
Speaking

Oral Practice

1. Suggest planning the family trip together.
"Let's plan our family trip together."
2. Suggest inviting all the relatives.
"Shall we invite all our relatives?"
3. Suggest visiting the grandparents.
"Why don't we visit our grandparents?"
4. Suggest going sightseeing on Sunday.
"How about going sightseeing on Sunday?"
5. Suggest checking the schedule and booking the hotel.
"Let's check the schedule and book the hotel."
6. Suggest planning the itinerary together.
"How about planning the itinerary together?"
Build

Fix and Improve

Make these suggestions correct:

"Let's to go sightseeing." ➔ "___Let's go sightseeing."
"How about visit the museum?" ➔ "___How about visiting the museum?"
"Shall we going to the beach?" ➔ "___Shall we go to the beach?"
Compare

Lesson 8 vs Lesson 9

Lesson 8: Need to / Have to

Obligations and health emergencies

"I need to see a doctor."

Lesson 9: Suggestions

Planning a family trip together

"Let's plan our family trip."

Mixed Practice

Prepare or Suggest?

Suggest planning the trip together: "___Let's plan our family trip together."
Say what you must do before traveling: "___I need to buy travel insurance."
Suggest inviting the relatives: "___Shall we invite all our relatives?"
Ask how to take your medicine: "___How do I have to take this medicine?"
Chapter 6: Production

Show What You Learned

Three levels of practice — guided, semi-free, and free

Role-Play

Role-Play: Planning Together

You are planning a family weekend trip with your sister. Make suggestions about who to invite and what to do.
Let'sShall werelativesgrandparentssightseeingschedule
Role-Play

Role-Play: The Itinerary

You are planning the itinerary with a cousin. Suggest the days and the activities for the trip.
itineraryWhy don't weHow aboutmuseum
Free Practice

Role-Play: The Family Reunion

Your whole family is meeting to plan a big reunion trip. You lead the planning: make suggestions, invite the relatives, and organize the itinerary.

Use everything you learned today: relatives, grandparents, cousins, nieces, nephews, schedule, sightseeing, itinerary, let's, shall we, why don't we, how about.

Feedback

Delayed Error Correction

Your teacher will share 3-5 corrections from the role-plays. Repeat each corrected version twice.

Teacher notes corrections here during the role-plays...

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

5 Key Phrases

1
"Let's plan our family trip together."
2
"Shall we invite all our relatives?"
3
"Why don't we visit our grandparents?"
4
"How about going sightseeing on Sunday?"
5
"Let's check the schedule and book the hotel."
Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can make suggestions with let's, shall we, why don't we, and how about.
I know 8 family and trip-planning words.
I use the base verb after let's and -ing after how about.
I can plan the activities of a family trip.
I can talk about my relatives (grandparents, cousins, nephews).
I can organize the schedule and the itinerary with my family.
I feel confident coordinating a family trip in English.

Family Planner

Badge earned! You can coordinate any family trip in English.

Lesson Complete

Day 9 — Complete!

Next lesson: Putting It All Together — Review and Confidence

Thank You

See you
next class!

Alumni by Better — Travel English

Lesson 10 — Final

Putting It
All Together

A2 — 60 minutes — Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Journey So Far

From Lesson 9 — Suggestions

Say these out loud — you used them to plan a family trip!

Let's"Let's plan our family trip together."
Shall we"Shall we invite all our relatives?"
Why don't we"Why don't we visit our grandparents?"
How about"How about going sightseeing on Sunday?"
Chapter 1: The Journey So Far

Ten Lessons,
One Confident Traveler

From freezing up to leading the trip

Today's Goal

After this lesson, Tânia will...

...handle a full trip from start to finish in English — combining every structure from the whole course with confidence.

Chapter 2: Words You Know

Your Travel
Vocabulary

Eight words, one from each part of the trip

Vocabulary

Review 1-4

Lesson 1 — you show it to travel
Passport
An official document to travel between countries
"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
Lesson 2 — it lets you board the plane
Boarding pass
The document that lets you get on the plane
"Could I see your boarding pass, please?"
Lesson 3 — you make one for a room or table
Reservation
When you book a room or table in advance
"I would like to check in. I have a reservation."
Lesson 4 — instructions to find a place
Directions
Instructions on how to get to a place
"Could you give me directions to the museum?"
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Review 5-8

Lesson 5 — the list of food in a restaurant
Menu
The list of food and drinks in a restaurant
"Could I have the menu, please?"
Lesson 6 — a gift you buy on a trip
Souvenir
A gift you buy to remember a trip
"How much is this souvenir?"
Lesson 7 — a famous place in a city
Landmark
A famous or important place in a city
"I have been to many famous landmarks."
Lesson 9 — the plan of a trip
Itinerary
The plan of places and times for a trip
"Let's plan the itinerary together."
0 / 4 words revealed
Review

Say It All

Click on each word to listen. You know every one.

Passport"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
Boarding pass"Could I see your boarding pass, please?"
Reservation"I would like to check in. I have a reservation."
Directions"Could you give me directions to the museum?"
Menu"Could I have the menu, please?"
Souvenir"How much is this souvenir?"
Landmark"I have been to many famous landmarks."
Itinerary"Let's plan the itinerary together."
Bridge

Every Structure, One Trip

You learned nine grammar structures. Today they all work together in one journey.

Chapter 3: The Code

All Your
Structures

Nine lessons of grammar, working together

Grammar Discovery

Where Is Each From?

Each sentence comes from a different lesson. Which structure is each one?

"Can you help me with my luggage?"
"I would like to check in."
"I have been to Portugal."
"I need to find a pharmacy."
"Let's go sightseeing."
StructureUseExample
Can / Couldrequests (L2)"Can you help me?"
Would likepolite wishes (L3)"I would like to check in."
Could I haveordering (L5)"Could I have the menu?"
Have been toexperiences (L7)"I have been to Portugal."
Need to / Have tonecessity (L8)"I need to find a pharmacy."
Let's / Shall wesuggestions (L9)"Let's go sightseeing."
Common Mistake

Watch Out!

"I go to many countries." (experience)
"I have been to many countries."
"I need find a pharmacy."
"I need to find a pharmacy."
Practice

Complete from Memory

"___Could I have the menu, please?"
"I ___would like to check in."
"I ___have been to many countries."
"I ___need to find a pharmacy."
"___Let's go sightseeing."
Build

Build from Any Lesson

Say the right sentence for each situation:

Show your passport: "___Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
Ask for directions: "___Could you tell me the way to the museum?"
Ask a price: "___How much is this souvenir?"
Suggest the next trip: "___Let's plan our next trip together."
Chapter 4: The Whole Trip

Tânia Tells
Her Story

The whole journey, in her own words

Artifact

Tânia's Course Passport

TRAVEL ENGLISH PASSPORT

Tânia Rosa — Course Complete

Can Do

Check in at airports and hotels · Ask for and follow directions · Order food and ask prices

Can Do

Talk about travel experiences · Handle health emergencies · Plan trips with family

Level

A2 — Independent traveler in English

Stamps

10 / 10 lessons completed

From freezing up to leading the trip — in 10 lessons.
Dialogue

How Was Your Trip?

F
So Tânia, you traveled alone in English! How was the airport?
T
Great! I said: Here is my passport. Can you help me with my luggage?
F
And the hotel?
T
I said: I would like to check in. I have a reservation.
F
How did you get around the city?
T
I asked: Could you tell me the way to the museum?
F
And the restaurant?
T
I said: Could I have the menu, please? The food was wonderful.
F
Did you have any problems?
T
Once I felt sick, so I said: I need to find a pharmacy. Let's plan the next trip together!
Comprehension

About the Conversation

1. What did Tânia say at the airport?
"Here is my passport. Can you help me with my luggage?"
2. How did Tânia ask for directions?
"Could you tell me the way to the museum?"
3. What did Tânia do when she felt sick?
She said: "I need to find a pharmacy."
Listening

Tânia, the Expert

Listen to Tânia helping a new traveler.

1. What directions does Tânia give?
"Go straight and turn left. The hotel is near the restaurant."
2. What does she say to do at a restaurant?
"Say: Could I have the menu, please?"
Listening

Tânia's Advice

Listen again. Count the phrases Tânia teaches.

1. What does Tânia say to do at the airport?
"Say: Here is my passport and my boarding pass."
2. What advice does she give about feeling sick?
"Say: I need to find a pharmacy. You will be fine!"
Chapter 5: Practice

Mixed
Practice

Quick fire, spot the error, and oral drilling

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

Click on each card to reveal the answer.

1. At check-in, show your passport.
"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
2. Ask the way to the hotel restaurant.
"Could you tell me where the restaurant is, please?"
3. Order food at a restaurant.
"Could I have the menu, please?"
4. Ask the price of a souvenir.
"How much is this souvenir?"
5. You feel sick. Find help.
"Excuse me, I need to find a pharmacy."
6. Suggest sightseeing with your family.
"Let's go sightseeing together."
Detective

Spot the Error

"I go to many countries." (experience)
"I have been to many countries."
"I need find a pharmacy."
"I need to find a pharmacy."
"I like to check in." (hotel)
"I would like to check in."
"Let's to go sightseeing."
"Let's go sightseeing."
"How much cost this?"
"How much is this?"
0 / 5 errors found
Speaking

Oral Practice

1. Show your passport at check-in.
"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
2. Check in at the hotel.
"I would like to check in, please."
3. Ask for directions to the museum.
"Could you tell me the way to the museum?"
4. Order at a restaurant.
"Could I have the menu, please?"
5. Handle a health problem.
"I need to find a pharmacy."
6. Suggest the next trip.
"Let's plan our next trip together."
Build

Fix and Improve

Make these sentences correct:

"I go to Spain." (experience) ➔ "___I have been to Spain."
"I need find pharmacy." ➔ "___I need to find a pharmacy."
"Let's to go sightseeing." ➔ "___Let's go sightseeing."
Compare

Lesson 1 vs Lesson 10

Lesson 1: First Day

Froze when English was needed

"I... travel... a lot?"

Lesson 10: Today

Leads a whole trip in English

"Here is my passport. Let's go!"

Mixed Practice

Any Situation, Right Words

Order food: "___Could I have the menu, please?"
Talk about experience: "___I have been to many countries."
Ask for help with bags: "___Can you help me with my luggage?"
Suggest a plan: "___Let's plan our next trip together."
Chapter 6: Production

The Full Trip Simulation

Three levels — guided, semi-free, and free

Role-Play

Role-Play: Airport & Hotel

You arrive at the airport and then check in at your hotel. Show your documents, ask for help, and check in.
Here is my passportCan you help meI would likereservation
Role-Play

Role-Play: City & Restaurant

You explore the city: ask for directions, order at a restaurant, and ask the price of a souvenir.
Could you tell me the wayCould I haveHow much
Free Practice

Role-Play: The Whole Trip

Travel alone from start to finish: check in, get around, eat, shop, handle a small emergency, and plan your next trip — all in English.

Use everything: passport, would like, could you tell me the way, could I have, how much, have been to, need to, let's.

Feedback

Delayed Error Correction

Your teacher will share a few corrections from the simulation. Repeat each corrected version twice.

Teacher notes corrections here during the simulation...

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

5 Phrases for Any Trip

1
"Here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
2
"I would like to check in, please."
3
"Could I have the menu, please?"
4
"I need to find a pharmacy."
5
"Let's plan our next trip together."
Self-Assessment

What I Can Do Now

I can check in at airports and hotels.
I can ask for and follow directions.
I can order food and ask prices.
I can talk about my travel experiences.
I can handle a health emergency abroad.
I can make suggestions and plan a trip.
I feel confident traveling and speaking English on my own.

Confident Traveler

Badge earned! You completed all 10 lessons of Travel English.

Course Complete

Course Complete!

10 / 10 lessons — you can travel the world in English

Thank You

Bon voyage,
Tânia!
